channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: DOGE's access to Treasury data risks US financial standing and raises security worries, experts warn
item.description: The Department of Government Efficiency’s embed into the federal government has raised a host of concerns, transforming a debate over how to cut government waste into a confrontation over privacy rights and the nation’s financial standing in the world.
item.dc:creator: Fatima Hussein And David Klepper, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Battleground Florida: Officials discuss tariffs, LGBTQ policies
item.description: University of Tampa Economics Professor Michael Coon explained the purpose of tariffs and the impact they will likely have on businesses and families in Tampa Bay.
item.dc:creator: Ty Russell, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Trump tariffs 'potentially very harmful' to US: John Bolton
item.description: President Donald Trump's ex-national security adviser said Trump's tariff plan could have harmful impacts on the economy.
item.dc:creator: Jeff Arnold, NewsNation

item.title: US cyber agency puts election security staffers who worked with the states on leave
item.description: Staffers at the nation’s cybersecurity agency whose job is to ensure the security of U.S. elections have been placed on administrative leave, jeopardizing critical support provided to state and local election offices across the country.
item.dc:creator: Christina A. Cassidy, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Scientists warn Trump's medical research cuts endanger patients as judge blocks the move for now
item.description: Scientists warn the Trump administration’s drastic cuts for medical research endanger patients, will cost thousands of jobs – and threaten America’s standing as a world leader in science and innovation.
item.dc:creator: LAURAN NEERGAARD and MICHAEL CASEY, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Trump fires Office of Government Ethics chief
item.description: President Trump has fired the director of the Office of Government Ethics, the agency announced Monday. The office posted on its website that it had been notified Trump was removing David Huitema, who had been nominated by former President Biden.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Vance triggers Dems by defending Trump's executive authority
item.description: Judges across the country took action to block President Donald Trump’s agenda. Vance created a social media frenzy on Sunday for supporting Trump’s executive authority.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: U.S. judge keeps his block on Trump federal buyout plan in place for now
item.description: A U.S. judge kept his block on President Donald Trump’s buyout plan for federal employees in place on Monday and said he would consider imposing it for a longer period of time.
item.dc:creator: MPR News

item.title: Palestinian refugees in Syria have a message for Gazans: Don’t leave your land
item.description: Palestinians fled the 1948 Mideast War and took refuge in neighboring Syria. After 77 years, they’re still waiting to go back. They are telling Palestinians in Gaza to stay put.
item.dc:creator: Greg Myre and Jawad Rizkallah, MPR News

item.title: Trump steps up his 2018 tariffs on steel and aluminum, risking inflation on promise of more jobs
item.description: President Donald Trump on Monday removed the exceptions and exemptions from his 2018 tariffs on steel, meaning that all steel imports will be taxed at a minimum of 25%.
item.dc:creator: Josh Boak, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Trump says he’s firing Kennedy Center board of trustees members and naming himself chair
item.description: President Donald Trump says he’s firing members of the board of trustees for the John F. Kennedy Center for the Performing Arts and naming himself chair.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: Why many Republicans think shrinking Medicaid will make it better
item.description: Republicans proposals to change the public health insurance program for low-income and disabled Americans could amount to more than $2 trillion of cuts over the next decade.
item.dc:creator: Ryan Levi, MPR News

item.title: Trump sued after quietly firing top whistleblower protector
item.description: President Trump late Friday fired the leader of an office designed to protect government whistleblowers, a detail revealed in a Monday lawsuit from the head of the Office of the Special Counsel (OSC).
item.dc:creator: Zach Schonfeld, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Judge orders fired head of whistleblower agency reinstated while fight continues over Trump removal
item.description: A federal judge has ordered the fired head of the federal agency dedicated to protecting whistleblowers to be reinstated while a court fight continues over his removal by President Donald Trump.
item.dc:creator: Alanna Durkin Richer And Eric Tucker, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Third judge blocks Trump’s order ending birthright citizenship for kids of people in US illegally
item.description: CONCORD, N.H. (AP) — A third federal judge on Monday blocked President Donald Trump’s executive order ending birthright citizenship for the children of people who are in the U.S. illegally. The ruling from U.S. District Judge Joseph N.
item.dc:creator: LINDSAY WHITEHURST and KATHY McCORMACK, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump says ceasefire deal should be canceled if Hamas doesn’t release all hostages by Saturday
item.description: President Donald Trump says that a precarious ceasefire agreement between Israel and Hamas should be canceled if Hamas doesn’t release all the remaining hostages it is holding in Gaza by midday on Saturday — though he also acknowledged that such a decision would be up to Israel.
item.dc:creator: Zeke Miller, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Trump says he will impose a 25% tariff on aluminum and steel imports
item.description: Additionally, he plans to announce reciprocal tariffs on "everyone" on Tuesday or Wednesday: "If they charge us, we charge them. That's all," he said. "Every country will be reciprocal.
item.dc:creator: NPR Staff, MPR News

item.title: A timeline of US-China tit-for-tat tariffs since Trump's first term
item.description: China’s tit-for-tat duties on U.S. imports took effect Monday, just hours after U.S. President Donald Trump announced that he wants to slap new duties on all steel and aluminum imports to the U.S.
item.dc:creator: SIMINA MISTREANU, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Kick start arrives for Minnesota lawmakers after standoff in session’s opening weeks
item.description: A push to roll out bills and organize committees comes after the House got started last week and the Senate ended a power-sharing deal.
item.dc:creator: Dana Ferguson, MPR News

item.title: Consumer Financial Protection Bureau to shut down for a week: Reports
item.description: A CFPB executive said in a Sunday email the agency headquarters would be closed from Monday to Friday.
item.dc:creator: Tara Suter, NewsNation

item.title: Trump defends Elon Musk as 'terrific' during Super Bowl interview
item.description: President Donald Trump calls federal courts blocking Elon Musk "crazy," and predicted the Kansas City Chiefs would win Super Bowl LIX.
item.dc:creator: Luke Garrett, MPR News

item.title: Vance and Musk question the authority of the courts as Trump's agenda faces legal pushback
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Top Trump administration officials are openly questioning the judiciary's authority to serve as a check on executive power as the new president's sweeping agenda faces growing pushback from the courts.
item.dc:creator: JILL COLVIN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump picks Chiefs for Super Bowl win, praising Mahomes
item.description: “I guess, you have to say that when a quarterback wins as much as he’s won, I have to go with Kansas City,” Trump told Fox News’s Bret Baier.
item.dc:creator: Alex Gangitano, NewsNation

item.title: New CFPB chief closes headquarters, tells all staff they must work remotely for the week
item.description: Vought was an architect of Project 2025. He takes over at the consumer finance watchdog as he orders a pause in most of the agency's activities, and promises to cut spending.
item.dc:creator: Laurel Wamsley, MPR News

item.title: How conspiracy theories about Politico led Trump to cancel subscriptions
item.description: Less than 24 hours after misleading claims started circulating that tied Politico to USAID, the White House vowed to end subscriptions for the insider news outlet's services and others.
item.dc:creator: Huo Jingnan and Jude Joffe-Block, MPR News

item.title: Undocumented people can feel safe to report crimes, Nassau County executive says
item.description: NEW YORK (PIX11) – The Nassau County Police Department will be directly participating in ICE enforcement to detain undocumented immigrants accused of crimes, County Executive Bruce Blakeman announced Tuesday. Nassau County announced that 10 detectives will work with ICE.
item.dc:creator: Henry Rosoff, PIX 11

item.title: Tampa Bay lawmaker files bill to allow state funds to be invested in Bitcoin
item.description: A Tampa Bay lawmaker filed a bill on Friday that would allow Florida's chief financial officer to invest state funds in Bitcoin.
item.dc:creator: Kevin Accettulla, WFLA, NBC

item.title: NYC Mayor's Race Poll: Cuomo the favorite, Adams looking stronger
item.description: NEW YORK (PIX11) -- An exclusive poll from PIX11 News, Emerson College, and The Hill shows former Gov. Andrew Cuomo would hold an early advantage if he hops in the New York City Democratic mayoral primary — but Mayor Eric Adams still has a chance.
item.dc:creator: Henry Rosoff, PIX 11

item.title: Poway City Council votes to censure councilmember over misconduct
item.description: The Poway City Council has voted to censure fellow councilmember Tony Blain over a string of inappropriate and unprofessional behavior.
item.dc:creator: Jennifer Franco, Fox 5

item.title: Gabbard, RFK Jr. pass committee hurdles in win for Trump
item.description: Tulsi Gabbard and Robert F. Kennedy Jr. on Tuesday cleared crucial hurdles toward winning Senate confirmation, handing President Trump a key win as he looks for an early display of loyalty from GOP lawmakers.
item.dc:creator: Al Weaver, Fox 5
